introduction: Failure to achieve conception after one year of regular, unprotected sexual intercourse in a couple trying to achieve pregnancy. Male factor is responsible for about 50% of infertile unions.

features: Ability to have erection, penetration and ejaculation Family history of infertility
History of systemic disease e.g. diabetes mellitus, hypertension, chronic liver disease and tuberculosis
History of sexually transmitted infections and urinary tract infections
History of surgery: herniorraphy, orchidopexy, urethral surgeries, etc
Absence of testis, small sized testis, varicocoeles, hard and irregular epididymis

investigations: Hormone profile (LH, FSH, testosterone, and prolactin)

goals: To improve semen quality and restore reproductive capability
non_drug: Transurethral resection of obstructed ejaculatory duct
